description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Glendale is a city located primarily in the Verdugo Mountains region, with a small portion in the San Fernando Valley of Los Angeles County, in the U.S. state of California.

description: professional art school (1921-1961)
description_wiki: The Chouinard Art Institute was a professional art school founded in 1921 by Nelbert Murphy Chouinard (1879–1969) in the Westlake neighborhood of Los Angeles, California.

description: American artist (1907-1989)
description_wiki: Millard Owen Sheets (June 24, 1907 – March 31, 1989) was an American artist, teacher, and architectural designer.

description: American artist-led organization to promote the advancement of watermedia art and artists
city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Beverly Hills is a city in Los Angeles County, California, United States.

aliases: School of the Chicago Art Institute
The School of the Art Institute of Chicago
description: university and independent school of art and design
description_wiki: The School of the Art Institute of Chicago (SAIC) is the private art school of the Art Institute of Chicago (AIC) in Chicago, Illinois.

description: Irish-American Union Army veteran, politician, and mobster
description_wiki: Lawrence Gustave Murphy (1831 – October 20, 1878) was an Irish immigrant to the United States, Union Army veteran, Grand Army of the Republic member, Republican Party ward heeler, racketeer, Old West businessman and gunman, and a main instigator of the Lincoln County War.

description: German-American painter (1880–1966)
description_wiki: Hans Hofmann (March 21, 1880 – February 17, 1966) was a German-born American painter, renowned as both an artist and teacher.

description: American sculptor and artist of Ukrainian origin (1887–1964)
description_wiki: Alexander Porfyrovych Archipenko (May 30 [O.S. May 18] 1887 – February 25, 1964) was a Ukrainian-American avant-garde artist, sculptor, and graphic artist, active in France and the United States.

description: Mexican painter and muralist (1908–1974)
description_wiki: David Alfaro Siqueiros (born José de Jesús Alfaro Siqueiros; December 29, 1896 – January 6, 1974) was a Mexican social realist painter, best known for his large public murals using the latest in equipment, materials and technique.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: 1926-1995 aerospace manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: The Lockheed Corporation was an American aerospace manufacturer.

description: Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Jepson Art Institute, founded in 1945 by artist Herbert Jepson, was an art school located at  2861 West 7th Street in the Westlake district of central Los Angeles, California.

description: art school in Los Angeles, California
description_wiki: Otis College of Art and Design is a private art and design school in Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: Italian artist (1900-1964)
description_wiki: Rico Federico Lebrun (December 10, 1900 – May 9, 1964) was an Italian-American painter and sculptor.

description: American painter (1920–1977)
American artist (1921-2008)
description_wiki: William Arnstein, professionally William Brice (April 23, 1921 – March 3, 2008) was an American artist known for his large-scale abstract paintings.

aliases: The United States Government as Represented by the Department of Veterans Affairs (Washington, DC)
description: department of the United States government
description_wiki: The United States Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) is a Cabinet-level executive branch department of the federal government charged with providing lifelong healthcare services to eligible military veterans at the 170 VA medical centers and outpatient clinics located throughout the country.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: American artist (1928–2023)
description_wiki: Robert Walter Irwin (September 12, 1928 – October 25, 2023) was an American installation artist who explored perception and the conditional in art, often through site-specific, architectural interventions that alter the physical, sensory and temporal experience of space.

description_wiki: The California Institute of the Arts (CalArts) is a private art school in Santa Clarita, California.

description: neighborhood of Santa Clarita, California, United States
description_wiki: Valencia is a neighborhood in Santa Clarita located within Los Angeles County, California.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.

description: cultural movement, starting around 1600
description_wiki: The Baroque (UK:  bə-ROK, US:  bə-ROHK, French: [baʁɔk]) is a Western style of architecture, music, dance, painting, sculpture, poetry, and other arts that flourished from the early 17th century until the 1750s.

description: Italian sculptor and architect (1598–1680)
description_wiki: Gian Lorenzo (or Gianlorenzo) Bernini (UK: , US: ; Italian: [ˈdʒan loˈrɛntso berˈniːni]; Italian Giovanni Lorenzo; 7 December 1598 – 28 November 1680) was an Italian sculptor, architect, painter and city planner.
